mcqs.question: What does the Fourier Transform of an image represent?

options.text: The frequency components that make up the image
The color distribution of the image

mcqs.explanation: The Fourier Transform decomposes an image into its constituent frequency components, showing how much of each frequency is present.

mcqs.question: Which property of Fourier Transform makes filtering operations more efficient in frequency domain?
mcqs.explanation: The convolution theorem states that convolution in spatial domain equals multiplication in frequency domain, making filtering operations more efficient.

mcqs.question: What is the computational complexity of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) for an N×N image?
mcqs.explanation: The 2D FFT has computational complexity of O(N² log N) for an N×N image, which is significantly better than the O(N⁴) complexity of the naive DFT implementation.

mcqs.question: In the frequency domain representation of an image, what does the phase spectrum primarily contain?
mcqs.explanation: The phase spectrum contains crucial structural information about the image. If you reconstruct an image using only phase information (with uniform magnitude), you can still recognize the original structure.

mcqs.question: Why is padding necessary when performing filtering operations using Fourier Transform?

options.text: To avoid wrap-around errors in circular convolution

mcqs.explanation: Padding is necessary to avoid wrap-around errors that occur due to the periodic nature of the Discrete Fourier Transform, which assumes the image is periodic.
